claim: Agent observability is critical for understanding what's happening under the hood in AI agents—it's one of the three core pillars of agent development alongside evaluations and iteration
actionable: Implement comprehensive logging and tracing for all agent operations including reasoning steps, tool calls, and token usage to enable debugging
source_title: AIE Singapore Day 2 ft. Google DeepMind, OpenClaw, Adaption, Arize, Cloudflare, Robot Company & more

claim: Staying on task is a core lesson learned from building production AI agents—it requires explicit mechanisms to keep agents focused on their assigned objective
actionable: Add task constraints and objective reminders at regular intervals in your agent prompts to prevent task drift
source_title: AIE Singapore Day 2 ft. Google DeepMind, OpenClaw, Adaption, Arize, Cloudflare, Robot Company & more

claim: Context management is a critical challenge when building agents—the agent must effectively manage and prioritize what context to use for each decision
actionable: Design context windows strategically, implement context pruning, and use relevance scoring to determine what information the agent should consider
source_title: AIE Singapore Day 2 ft. Google DeepMind, OpenClaw, Adaption, Arize, Cloudflare, Robot Company & more

claim: Crystallizing good behavior in agents requires using evaluations and performance data to identify and reinforce successful patterns
actionable: Run regular evaluations on agent outputs, analyze what patterns lead to success, and encode those patterns back into prompts or training
source_title: AIE Singapore Day 2 ft. Google DeepMind, OpenClaw, Adaption, Arize, Cloudflare, Robot Company & more

claim: Agents should be able to select and manage their own compute resources—deciding when to do work and at what inference time, rather than humans managing this ahead of time
actionable: Design agent architectures that allow dynamic model selection, compute scheduling, and workload optimization based on token profiles
source_title: AIE Singapore Day 2 ft. Google DeepMind, OpenClaw, Adaption, Arize, Cloudflare, Robot Company & more

claim: Token profiles (predicted token distribution) are crucial for inference optimization—providers that know a session's token profile beforehand can better allocate compute and reduce failures
actionable: Work with inference providers to establish token profile expectations upfront, or implement prediction mechanisms to forecast token usage patterns
source_title: AIE Singapore Day 2 ft. Google DeepMind, OpenClaw, Adaption, Arize, Cloudflare, Robot Company & more

claim: Debugging real agents in production requires specialized tooling for observability—understanding not just what outputs agents produce but why they made specific decisions
actionable: Build or use agent debugging tools that track decision trees, tool invocations, and reasoning steps end-to-end
source_title: AIE Singapore Day 2 ft. Google DeepMind, OpenClaw, Adaption, Arize, Cloudflare, Robot Company & more
